mechanism_narrative: Growth in industrial production signals expanding manufacturing output and rising corporate profits, which positively predicts S&P 500 returns over the next month as earnings expectations improve.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns do not show positive correlation with INDPRO growth over any 3-year rolling window; Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 4% signals labor market weakening, which reduces consumer spending and earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 4%, 20-day forward returns are not negative in at least 60% of instances over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising federal funds rates and elevated consumer price inflation tightens financial conditions and depresses equity valuations, jointly producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term of FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL fails to produce significant negative coefficient in 20d forward return regressions (p>0.05) over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles defined by the federal funds rate rising above its 12-month moving average, the restrictive policy stance compresses equity valuations and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle: FEDFUNDS above 12-month MA and rising
failure_condition: During tightening cycles, 60-day forward returns are not significantly lower than during other regimes (t-test p < 0.05) over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month drop in retail sales signals a sudden contraction in consumer demand, triggering immediate negative investor sentiment and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: When RSAFS drops >1% MoM, 5-day forward returns are not negative in at least 55% of cases over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained high levels of the consumer price index erode real purchasing power and raise the risk of further monetary tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Sustained negative correlation between CPIAUCSL level and 20-day forward returns fails to produce Rank IC < -0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Falling housing starts combined with rising federal funds rates amplifies the negative impact on residential investment and consumer wealth, jointly predicting weaker S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint effect of rising FEDFUNDS and declining HOUST does not predict negative 60-day returns with significant interaction coefficient (p>0.10) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a tight labor market with unemployment below 4%, any subsequent rise in the unemployment rate signals the start of a weakening cycle, which hurts corporate earnings and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 4%, increase in UNRATE does not lead to negative 20-day returns in > 55% of cases over rolling 5-year windows.
